changes_made: Renamed k8s_networkpolicy_enforce_secure_transport to k8s_networkpolicy_secure_transport_enforced for consistency with naming conventions
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control's focus on secure information flow. The naming convention was slightly adjusted for the network policy function to better match the pattern and improve clarity. The other functions are appropriately named and map well to Kubernetes security features.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names use past tense for actions to maintain consistency, e.g., 'enforced' instead of 'enforce'.
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | modify non - releasable information when transfer
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(23) focuses on ensuring secure information flow, particularly modifying non-releasable information during transfer. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring secure transport of data through various mechanisms. NetworkPolicies can enforce secure transport by restricting traffic to only use secure protocols. Ingress resources should have TLS enabled to ensure secure data transfer. Service meshes like Istio or Linkerd can enforce mTLS for secure communication between services. Admission controllers can be used to enforce policies that ensure secure transport configurations are in place.
